highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 adds Vulkan/Silk.NET support, FreeType on Nano Server, and several performance fixes.
highlights_body: This release expands platform reach with a new Vulkan binding package and FreeType-backed font support on Windows Nano Server. It also includes a memory-leak fix in raster image creation and two performance improvements in text encoding and runtime-effect uniforms.

bullets.detail: A dedicated NuGet package integrates SkiaSharp with Vulkan via the Silk.NET bindings, enabling GPU-accelerated rendering on Linux and Android Vulkan back-ends.

bullets.lead: FreeType font support on Windows Nano Server
bullets.detail: Nano Server containers lack the GDI font stack; SkiaSharp now falls back to FreeType so font rendering works in that environment.

bullets.lead: Memory leak in SKImage.Create raster failure path fixed
bullets.detail: A CoTaskMem pixel buffer was not freed when the underlying Skia raster-image creation failed, causing a native memory leak. The buffer is now released on the failure path.

bullets.lead: Avoid intermediate string allocation in GetEncodedText null-terminated path
bullets.detail: The hot path that reads null-terminated text no longer allocates an intermediate string, reducing GC pressure on text-heavy workloads.

bullets.lead: Pre-size uniform lookup map in SKRuntimeEffectUniforms
bullets.detail: The uniform-name lookup dictionary is now sized to the known count at construction time, eliminating rehash allocations for typical runtime-effect usage.
All .sln solution files have been migrated to the lighter .slnx format for improved tooling compatibility.

bullets.lead: Containerized test legs added: Azure Linux, Alpine/musl, Nano Server
bullets.detail: CI now runs a full test matrix on Azure Linux, Alpine (musl libc), and Windows Nano Server containers, catching platform-specific regressions earlier.

contributor_summaries.jeffska: FreeType font support on Windows Nano Server, fixing long-standing font rendering failures in container environments.
